question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that NetworkStatus() returns CurrentBlockTimestamp via currentBlock.GetTimestampMillis() which divides ConsensusStartNanos by time.Millisecond, and if ConsensusStartNanos is 0 or negative (edge case in genesis), receive a misleading or zero timestamp that confuses client-side chain validation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the context passed to RetrieveGenesis, RetrieveLatest, and Entries in NetworkStatus() is the HTTP request context, and by sending a slow-loris style request with a very long timeout, hold database connections open for extended periods?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the ErrEndpointNotSupportedInOfflineMode error by calling /network/status when the service is in offline mode, and use the error code (136) and message to confirm the server's operational mode for further targeted attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RetrieveGenesis uses a sync.Once internally in blockRepository, and if the first call to initGenesisRecordFile fails, the once.Do block is never executed, causing all subsequent NetworkStatus calls to re-attempt genesis initialization on every request, creating a persistent performance degradation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the three-phase sequential DB access in NetworkStatus() (RetrieveGenesis, RetrieveLatest, Entries) to perform a timing side-channel attack, measuring response latency to infer database load and chain synchronization state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NewNetworkAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that operationTypes is built once at service construction time via GetStringValuesFromInt32StringMap(types.GetTransactionTypes()), and if OperationTypeFee already exists in the transaction types map, it gets appended again, causing duplicate entries in the OperationTypes list returned by NetworkOptions()?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NewNetworkAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the network *rTypes.NetworkIdentifier pointer passed to NewNetworkAPIService() is stored directly in networkAPIService.network without copying, so if the caller modifies the NetworkIdentifier after construction, the service's network identity is silently corrupted, causing NetworkList() to return wrong data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NewNetworkAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the version *rTypes.Version pointer is stored by reference in networkAPIService.version, and if the caller modifies the Version struct after construction, NetworkOptions() returns a corrupted version string to all clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user call /network/options and use the returned Version.RosettaVersion and Version.NodeVersion fields to fingerprint the exact software version running, enabling targeted exploitation of known version-specific vulnerabilities?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that peers.ToRosetta() in address_book_entry.go formats PeerID as fmt.Sprintf(\

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the metadata map in ToRosetta() which includes raw endpoint strings from the database (IP:port format from strings.Split), and if the database contains endpoint strings with embedded special characters or injection payloads, cause JSON injection or log injection in downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NetworkOptions() builds operationStatuses by calling persistence.IsTransactionResultSuccessful(value) for each transaction result code, and if this function has side effects or panics on unexpected codes, cause a server crash?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkList()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NetworkList() returns the same n.network pointer in every response, and under concurrent access, if the Rosetta SDK framework modifies the returned NetworkIdentifier (e.g., adding metadata), cause a data race on the shared network struct?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Entries() call in NetworkStatus() queries both address book file IDs (101 and 102) sequentially in the underlying repository, and by timing the response, determine which address book file is active on the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user call /network/options repeatedly to force the server to re-iterate the entire transactionResults map (up to MaxTransactionResult=337 entries) on every request, causing CPU waste proportional to the map size under high request rates?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the context.Context passed to NetworkStatus() is the raw HTTP request context without any additional timeout wrapping, allowing a client to set an extremely long HTTP timeout and hold server resources indefinitely across all three DB calls?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RetrieveLatest() in blockRepository uses selectLatestWithIndex which does ORDER BY index DESC LIMIT 1 without index hints, and under high write load, cause this query to perform a full table scan, amplifying the DoS impact of repeated /network/status calls?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-deterministic map iteration order in GetTransactionResults() to cause the OperationStatuses slice to have different orderings across requests, breaking client-side caching or comparison logic that assumes stable ordering?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NetworkStatus() returns the GenesisBlockIdentifier with its hash prefixed by SafeAddHexPrefix(), and if the genesis block hash is empty, receive a response with a bare '0x' hash that confuses downstream block validation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that currentBlock.GetTimestampMillis() performs integer division (ConsensusStartNanos / int64(time.Millisecond)) without overflow checking, and if ConsensusStartNanos is math.MinInt64, cause an integer overflow producing a negative timestamp in the response?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Errors field in the NetworkOptionsResponse is the global errors.Errors slice (not a copy), and if the Rosetta SDK or middleware modifies the returned slice, corrupt the global error registry for all future NetworkOptions() calls?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() in the persistence layer uses string_agg to concatenate endpoints with commas, and if an endpoint string itself contains a comma, cause incorrect endpoint parsing in strings.Split(n.Endpoints, \

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Peers field in NetworkStatusResponse is built from all address book entries without any size limit, and if the address book contains thousands of entries, cause the server to allocate and serialize a multi-megabyte response, exhausting server memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user use the complete OperationTypes list from /network/options to discover that OperationTypeFee ('FEE') is a valid operation type, and attempt to craft construction requests that include FEE operations to manipulate fee accounting?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: '] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the error returned by addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() is ErrDatabaseError (code 125, retriable=true), and use the retriable flag to justify automated retry storms against /network/status when the database is degraded, amplifying the outage?
